name: God's Granola
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon cayenne, or less to taste
1 cup raw peanuts, without the papery skins
1 teaspoon black mustard seeds, see notes
1 pinch ground asafetida, optional, see notes
1 1/2 teaspoons ground amchoor, optional (see notes), or dried mango, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir together the salt, cinnamon, cayenne, pepper and cloves.
Place aside.
2. Line two cookie sheets with several thicknesses of paper towel. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F. Have a sieve handy.
3. CHICKPEAS: In a wok, deep skillet or other deep-frying vessel, pour enough corn oil to reach a depth of 1 inch. Heat until the oil is hot enough to make a dropped-in chick-pea sizzle immediately. Then put the chick-peas into a sieve and set the sieve carefully into the oil. Cook until the chick-peas are sizzling all over. Drain them on half of the paper-towel-lined cookie sheet.
4. FRUITS: Fry 2 tablespoons of the golden raisins, 2 tablespoons of the dark raisins, and 2 tablespoons of the dried cherries in the same way. Cook until they puff up like little spheres. Drain the first sieveful on part of the first cookie sheet and repeat the process with the remaining 2 tablespoons of each of the dried fruits.
5. NUTS: Put the cashews and peanuts on a large baking pan or rimmed cookie sheet. Take out 2 tablespoons of the "raisin oil" and stir into the nuts. Bake the nuts for 10-12 minutes, stirring frequently, until they are golden and cooked through. Drain them on the second lined cookie sheet.
6. SPICED SESAME SEEDS: In a small skillet over low heat, heat the remaining 1 1/2 tablespoons oil. When it's hot, put in the mustard seeds and the optional asafetida. Stir these constantly for a few seconds until the seeds begin to pop. Then add the sesame seeds and stir them constantly until they're golden brown. Add the turmeric, stir for 3 seconds and take the skillet off the heat.
7. FINAL INGREDIENTS: In a large bowl, stir together the fried spices and their oil, the ingredients on the cookie sheets, the Rice Krispies, the dried spices from the beginning of this recipe, the sugar and optional amchoor or dried mango. Toss everything together well. Cool the granola thoroughly, give it a final toss and store it airtight at room temperature.
8. Makes about 6 cups.
Notes: The chick-peas, black mustard seeds, and asafetida are available at Indian stores, some specialty shops and some health-food stores. Amchoor is dried, powdered mango, available at Indian stores. Dried mango is available at health food stores. This mix is also known as Asian Ambrosia, Posy's Cheewrah, or St. John's Trail Mix. From Ann Hodgman's BEAT THAT! COOKBOOK, 1995, Chapters Publishing Ltd., ISBN 1-881527-92-1, pages 21-23.
